rationale: The cited paper studies a highly similar dynamic—an option that is initially available and then becomes unavailable—and its impact on subsequent choice shares and mechanisms (attribute-weight/similarity shifts). The citing paper’s core phenomenon is also post-unavailability choice change after anticipated access to an aspirant option, so the cited work is plausibly a mechanism/theory or direct comparison source rather than a generic mention. With this conceptual overlap and no clear evidence of incidental use, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
